name: Ocejo and San Lorenzo Ascent Trail
name_original: Refugio Prao Tajo, Mostajo del Río Cárdenas, Cueva del Santo  – circular
description: This difficult 22.4-mile mountain biking loop explores the Sierra de la Demanda, featuring ancient trees, panoramic views, and historic site
region_intro: Mountain biking around Berceo offers diverse terrain in the lower Sierra de La Demanda, characterized by mountains and varied forests. The region features a mix of beeches, oaks, holm oaks, pines, and junipers, providing shaded routes and varied riding conditions. Situated between the Cárdenas and Tuerto rivers, the area presents a landscape suitable for challenging ascents and descents. This natural setting provides a foundation for a range of mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Berceo The most popular mountain bike trail is Refugio Prao Tajo, Mostajo del Río Cárdenas, Cueva del Santo – circular, a 22.4 miles (36.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Cárdenas River valley and passes by the Prao Tajo refuge. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is San Millan de la Cogolla, Badarán – circular por Cañas, a difficult 29.3 miles (47.2 km) path. This route offers expansive views and connects several local villages. Local mountain bikers also love the Fuente La Teja – Bonicaparra Shelter loop from San Millán de la Cogolla, a 30.9 miles (49.7 km) trail leading through forested mountain terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Berceo is defined by diverse forests, river valleys, and the rugged terrain of the Sierra de La Demanda, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Berceo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 260 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Berceo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Berceo?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Berceo, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored more than 58 routes in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Berceo?
A: Mountain biking in Berceo offers diverse terrain within the lower Sierra de La Demanda. You'll encounter mountains and varied forests, including beeches, oaks, holm oaks, pines, and junipers. The region is situated between the Cárdenas and Tuerto rivers, providing a landscape suitable for both challenging ascents and descents through river valleys and shaded routes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Berceo?
A: Yes, Berceo offers a good selection of challenging trails for experienced mountain bikers. There are 24 difficult routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993871771/refugio-prao-tajo-mostajo-del-rio-cardenas-cueva-del-santo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Prao Tajo, Mostajo del Río Cárdenas, Cueva del Santo – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.4-mile (36.0 km) trail exploring the Cárdenas River valley, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993747874/san-millan-de-la-cogolla-badaran-circular-por-canas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millan de la Cogolla, Badarán – circular por Cañas&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult 29.3-mile (47.2 km) path with expansive views.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Berceo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Berceo are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993871771/refugio-prao-tajo-mostajo-del-rio-cardenas-cueva-del-santo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Prao Tajo, Mostajo del Río Cárdenas, Cueva del Santo – circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente La Teja – Bonicaparra Shelter loop from San Millán de la Cogolla&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through forested mountain terrain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Berceo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Berceo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 260 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse forests and scenic river valleys.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Berceo?
A: The diverse forests around Berceo, with beeches, oaks, and pines, offer beautiful scenery throughout the seasons. Spring and autumn are generally ideal for mountain biking due to milder temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summer can also be pleasant, especially on shaded routes, but it's advisable to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural features to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region around Berceo is rich in both natural and cultural landmarks. You can find natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mostajo of the Cárdenas River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3266634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente La Teja&lt;/a&gt;. Culturally, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suso and Yuso Monasteries (San Millán de la Cogolla)&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, are significant attractions.

Q: Is parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Berceo?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not detailed, Berceo and nearby villages like San Millán de la Cogolla and Badarán, which serve as starting points for many routes, typically offer parking options. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Berceo?
A: Yes, there are a few easier mountain bike trails available in Berceo, with 3 routes categorized as easy. These trails are generally less strenuous and can be a good starting point for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride through the scenic landscape.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Berceo?
A: The region around Berceo is generally welcoming to outdoor activities. While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails are not provided, it's common practice in natural areas to keep dogs on a leash, especially in forested areas or near wildlife. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

Q: What cultural sites can I visit near the mountain biking routes?
A: The region is home to significant cultural sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suso and Yuso Monasteries in San Millán de la Cogolla&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, are particularly notable. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4598570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Valvanera&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of outdoor activity and historical exploration.
tips:
This refuge is located at the end of the paved track that goes up the Cárdenas river valley from San Millán de la Cogolla. The shelter has two rooms, one of them having a fireplace and a table.

It is a usual starting point for the ascent to the San Lorenzo peak, summit of the Sierra de la Demanda and the highest point in La Rioja.

This mostajo or rowan is located a few meters from a path that runs through the Sierra de la Demanda. Rowans are small trees that rarely reach fifteen meters in height. However, this is a rowan tree to be highlighted, probably one of the largest known specimens from the Peninsula and surely one of the longest-lived.

The Cueva del Santo is open to the public, since the door does not have a key. It is important to leave the site as we found it. According to legend, once upon a time a plague affected the women of the valley, for this reason the men approached the cave to ask the saint for his intermediation, thus beginning the tradition of only men going up on pilgrimage every year to the Cueva del Santo.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this circular route is rated as difficult and requires a very good level of physical fitness and advanced riding skills. The technical difficulty ranges from S3 to S6, indicating significant challenges, and some sections may even require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The trail features a highly varied terrain. You'll encounter a mix of paths, unpaved tracks, asphalt sections, gravel roads, natural paths, and narrow trails. Be prepared for rocky sections and steep inclines, typical of a challenging mountain biking experience in the Sierra de la Demanda.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll ride through picturesque landscapes of the Sierra de la Demanda. The route includes a beautiful, dense, and shady beech forest (hayedo del río Cárdenas), along with hazelnut, ash, holly, and maple trees. You'll also pass through bucolic meadows and see small waterfalls in the Barranco de Escarzuela. Don't miss the panoramic vistas from the "Curva de los Cuatro Vientos" overlooking the valley and prominent peaks.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the Refugio Prao Tajo, the unique and ancient Mostajo of the Cárdenas River (a cataloged rowan tree), and the historic Cueva del Santo (Sancti Emiliani), where San Millán de la Cogolla lived as a hermit. Adjacent to the cave is a hermitage offering breathtaking views.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the exact trailhead are not provided, access points near the Cueva del Santo or Refugio Prao Tajo are likely starting points. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's route planner for the most convenient parking areas closest to your intended start.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in the Sierra de la Demanda. However, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the varied terrain, potential wildlife, and the presence of other trail users. Always practice responsible pet ownership and pack out any waste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The route is particularly vibrant and beautiful in spring. Autumn is also highly recommended, as the changing colors of the beech forest and other deciduous trees make the scenery especially spectacular. Be mindful of weather conditions, especially in winter, as higher elevations may experience snow or ice.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for mountain biking in the Sierra de la Demanda?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the broader region of Sierras de la Demanda, Urbión, Cebollera y Cameros. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails in this area. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sierras Riojanas, Altos Valles Ibéricos, Pista de subida al Ocejo, Pista a San Lorenzo, Sendero GR, Urre, Camino Río Cabañares, Camino del Rajao a Pico los Randos, and Camino de Ocijo.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of the Cueva del Santo?
faqs.answer: The Cueva del Santo is a significant historical and religious site. It's where San Millán de la Cogolla, the patron saint of the region, lived as a hermit for 40 years. An ancient tradition involves men making a pilgrimage to this cave, which is adjacent to a hermitage offering stunning views.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or panoramic spots along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Curva de los Cuatro Vientos" offers excellent panoramic vistas of the valley and prominent peaks like Randos, Pancrudos, or Cabeza Parda. Additionally, the hermitage adjacent to the Cueva del Santo provides breathtaking views of the Cárdenas river valley and the surrounding Sierra de la Demanda mountains.
